question_text: When white light passes through a prism, it splits into different colours. Put these colours in the correct order from red to violet: Green, Indigo, Orange, Red, Violet, Yellow, Blue
correct_answer: Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Blue, Indigo, Violet (ROYGBIV)
explanation: When white light disperses through a prism, the colours always separate in the same order because each colour has a different wavelength. Red has the longest wavelength and violet has the shortest, and this order is remembered as ROYGBIV.
